faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard protocol that lets AI models connect to external tools — enabling Claude to interact with on-chain data, exchange APIs, and more.
faq.a2: All Skills are completely free. You just need a Claude Desktop account.
faq.q3: What if I run into issues?
faq.a3: Join our Telegram community — someone will help you troubleshoot.

costEstimate.title: Monthly Cost Estimates
costEstimate.subtitle: Not sure what it'll cost? See your monthly bill in 3 seconds.
costEstimate.lightDesc: A few chats a day — asking questions, looking things up. Like casually Googling stuff.
costEstimate.mediumDesc: Daily work use — writing copy, checking data, running Cron jobs. Your AI is basically a coworker now.
costEstimate.heavyDesc: All-day dev mode — coding, building projects, multi-agent parallel work. AI is your entire team.
costEstimate.vpsDesc: If you want Cron jobs running 24/7 without your computer on, you'll need a cloud server. Costs roughly $5-15/month. If you only use it on your own machine, skip this.

savingTips.title: Money-Saving Tips
savingTips.tip1: Sonnet for daily work, Haiku for Cron.
savingTips.tip1Desc: Sonnet handles 90% of tasks. Haiku runs scheduled jobs perfectly at a fraction of the cost.
savingTips.tip2Desc: Only switch for deep reasoning (complex code, long-form analysis). Most people are fine with Sonnet.
savingTips.tip3Desc: If you work mainly in Chinese, DeepSeek matches the big players at 1/20th the price.

guide.title: Beginner's Guide
guide.subtitle: Follow along — master the full AI Skills workflow in 4 days

day1.label: Day 1
day1.title: Install + Join Telegram
day1.subtitle: Your AI assistant, live in 5 minutes. No coding background needed. No docs to read.
day1.step1Desc: Claude Code runs on Node.js. You don't need to know what it is — just install it.
day1.step2Desc: Three options. Pick one. Beginners: go with the one-click install.
day1.step3Desc: Grab a key from your preferred provider. Recommended: Anthropic Claude ($5 free credits), DeepSeek (dirt cheap), Google Gemini (free tier).
day1.step4Desc: Navigate to any project folder, run claude and start chatting. On first launch, Claude generates a CLAUDE.md — your project's briefing file.
day1.step5Desc: Talk to your AI without opening a computer. Once set up, you can ask questions and receive notifications right from your phone.

troubleshootItems.a1: Install the latest with nvm: nvm install 22 && nvm use 22. System package managers often ship outdated versions.
troubleshootItems.q2: Windows can't find node after install?
troubleshootItems.a2: Close and reopen your terminal. The PATH needs a restart to take effect.
troubleshootItems.q3: EACCES permission error during npm install?
troubleshootItems.a3: Don't use sudo npm install. Switch to nvm for Node.js to avoid permission issues entirely.
troubleshootItems.a4: Open PowerShell as Admin, run: Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser

day2.label: Day 2
day2.title: Install 3 Essential Skills
day2.subtitle: An AI without Skills is just a chatbot. Install Skills and it goes from "chatbot" to "actual assistant." Install 3 today, tomorrow you'll want 30.
day2.step1Desc: 13.4% of community Skills have security issues. Before installing anything else, get your defenses up. Environment audit + malicious Skill blocking + runtime monitoring.
day2.step2Desc: 418.6K installs — one of the most popular Skills. When you don't know what Skill to use, just ask it. No need to memorize hundreds of names.
day2.step3Desc: Trader → CoinAnk API (K-lines, ETF flows, whale tracking). News → BlockBeats (research reports, 1500+ sources). Binance user → Binance Skills Hub (7-in-1). Not sure → go with Binance, most versatile.
day2.step4Desc: Before installing any new Skill, make this a habit: scan with AgentGuard. When in doubt, don't install — better to miss a feature than gain a vulnerability.

troubleshootItems.a1: Make sure Node.js 20+ is installed. Check with: node -v
troubleshootItems.a2: You need to restart Claude Code to load new Skills.
troubleshootItems.a3: Two channels: HunterKit Skills Directory (170+ curated), find-skills (just ask it — searches the community marketplace, always scan with AgentGuard first).

day3.label: Day 3
day3.title: Set Up Your First Cron
day3.subtitle: Make your AI proactive. Cron in plain English: scheduled alarm + auto-execution. Without Cron = AI waits for you. With Cron = AI works for you.
day3.step1Desc: Prerequisites: Telegram from Day 1 + CoinAnk or Binance Skill. Just tell Claude what you want in natural language — it converts the cron expression for you.
day3.step2Desc: Don't just check prices. ETH funding rate every 2 hours, news digest at 6 PM daily, portfolio report every Monday, whale transfer alerts daily.
day3.step3Desc: Cron results are only useful if they reach you. If you set up Telegram in Day 1, results auto-push. Test it by asking Claude to send a message.

troubleshootItems.a1: Make sure Claude Code stays open. For 24/7 operation, you'll need a VPS ($5-15/month).
troubleshootItems.a2: Go back to Day 1 Step 5 to verify Telegram Bot setup. Tell Claude: "Test Telegram push — send me a Hello from AI message" to confirm the connection.

week2.label: Week 2
week2.title: Advanced Configuration
week2.subtitle: From "it works" to "it works great." You already have an AI that chats, has skills, and runs tasks. This week: smarter, cheaper, more powerful.
week2.step1Title: Customize SOUL.md — Give AI a Personality
week2.step1Desc: SOUL.md is your AI's character sheet. Without it, generic assistant. With it, responds to your preferences, style, and needs. Come back and tweak it over time.
week2.step2Desc: Not every task needs the most expensive model. Use Sonnet ($3/M) for daily work, Haiku ($0.25/M) for Cron tasks, Opus only for deep analysis. Most people spend $5-10/month.
week2.step3Desc: AI is like a super-intelligent person locked in a windowless room. MCP opens windows. Each Skill you install opens a new window. You don't need to understand the technical details.
week2.step4Desc: You don't need to code — let Claude do it. Describe what you want, Claude generates the code, scan with AgentGuard, set up a Cron. If you can describe it, you can build it.

troubleshootItems.a1: Make sure the file is in the project root and restart Claude Code. Be specific — don't just write "help me", tell AI your role, preferences, and focus areas.
troubleshootItems.a2: Switch API keys and model params in Claude Code settings. Different providers use different env vars: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY.
